text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE


Clinic Date: 22 September 2023 

Dr Wang
Saffron Park Hospital

Saffron Park, London, E14 7JL
Dear Dr Wang
John Doe, DOB: 21-11-1982, Hospital No: P546484 NHS No. 5484656746
Flat 15 Roundwood Road, London, E14 7JL 

I reviewed the above patient in the Neurology Clinic today. He works as a warehouse picker, moving between tall racking in narrow aisles with variable lifting demands and shift-dependent pace. He reports that periods of higher workload and insufficient rest correlate with feeling “on edge,” light-headed, and more forgetful at the end of shifts.

Since our last contact he has been maintained on Levetiracetam 1 g b.d. and Lamotrigine 150 mg b.d. He takes Clobazam 10 mg nocte as needed for sleep disruption linked to alternating early/late shifts; he estimates using this on two to three nights per week. He denies rash, diplopia, or overt mood lability, but does describe intermittent word-finding difficulty and post-shift fatigue. No history of head injury at work. He adheres to dosing, carrying a phone reminder when on nights.

In terms of recent events, he has kept a brief diary and highlights the following: Seizure events on 03-09, 04-15, 05-04, 06-20, 06-23, 09-17. He reports each event as a sudden blank spell with loss of awareness, afterwards feeling confused and “heavy” for 30–60 minutes. Two events (06-20 and 09-17) occurred at work; colleagues noted he became still and did not respond to questions, with no witnessed convulsion or incontinence. He has not driven. He relates increased frequency to consecutive long shifts and poor sleep on changeover days. There have been no tongue bites or injuries. 

Neurological examination today is non-focal. Bloods from August 2023 showed normal FBC, U&E, LFTs; Vitamin D borderline low at 24 nmol/L. He continues with standard seizure first aid education and has informed his employer. Occupational risk remains pertinent due to ladder use and work among moving pallet trucks; he is avoiding working at height pending further advice from occupational health.

Plan: 
- Continue Levetiracetam 1 g b.d. and Lamotrigine 150 mg b.d. for now. 
- Arrange ambulatory EEG within the next 6–8 weeks to further characterise events and guide management. 
- Routine MRI brain (epilepsy protocol) as previous imaging is unavailable in our system. 
- Letter to occupational health supporting temporary avoidance of work at height and recommending buddy support in narrow aisles until monitoring is complete. 
- Sleep hygiene advice discussed; he will aim for more consistent bedtimes across shift transitions. 
- Safety reinforced: no driving and standard precautions around machinery and water.

I will review him after investigations or sooner if events cluster or change in character. Please let me know if there are concerns from your side.

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly lists recent seizure events with dates: 03-09, 04-15, 05-04, 06-20, 06-23, 09-17. These are six events in 2023. The clinic date is 22 September 2023. The dates appear to be month-day format (common in clinic notes listing events within the same year): March 9, April 15, May 4, June 20, June 23, and September 17. Counting from March through September gives a 7-month span (March, April, May, June, July, August, September up to 22nd). Frequency calculation: 6 events over approximately 6 months. Normalized to allowed format: '6 per 6 month'.
